questions.question: What is the primary benefit of a 'Rolling' 12-week calendar versus a static annual plan?

options.text: It requires less work to set up initially
It allows you to stay relevant and adapt to market shifts while maintaining a long-term horizon
It is easier to print out
A 12-week plan is always more accurate than a 4-week plan

questions.explanation: High-level placeholders for the future (Weeks 9-12) keep you strategic, while the rolling Friday update keeps the immediate tasks (Weeks 1-4) realistic.

questions.question: In the 12-week framework, what characterizes 'The Red Zone' (Weeks 1-4)?

options.text: High detail: specific headlines, outlines, and deadlines
Vague ideas you might write about someday
Content you have already deleted
Ideas from your competitors

questions.explanation: The Red Zone is for immediate execution. It must be detailed enough that you can start writing without additional 'planning' work.

questions.question: What is the purpose of rotating your content pillars weekly?

options.text: To keep the founder from getting bored
To ensure you build authority across all your 3-5 core topics without ignoring one
To trick the social media algorithms
To avoid having to write long-form content

questions.explanation: Rotation ensures balanced growth of your topical authority. It prevents you from only posting about the 'easiest' topic.

questions.question: Why is 'Batching' recommended for solo founders?

options.text: It saves money on software
It allows you to stay in 'Deep Work' by performing similar tasks together rather than switching contexts
It makes your website look better
Batching is only for video content

questions.explanation: Context switching is expensive for solo founders. Researching 4 pieces at once is faster than doing it 4 separate times across the month.

questions.question: When is the recommended time to 'Roll' the content calendar forward for next week?

options.text: Monday morning right before writing
Every Friday afternoon/evening
Once a month
Only when you run out of ideas

questions.explanation: Performing the planning work on Friday ensures that when you sit down to write on Monday, the decision-making is already done and you can focus 100% on execution.

questions.question: Look at your upcoming Week 1 and Week 2. What specific 'Source Piece' title will you write for each? Which Pillar does each belong to? What day will you block off as your 'Batching Day' next week?
questions.promptForAI: Review if the founder is being specific with their headlines. Look for the commitment to a 'Batching Day' as a sign of systematic execution.
